cards.sentence: I'm throwing everything against the wall. I'm looking at what's going to stick. I am looking to try to find the winning tactic and turn it into a winning strategy.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 될 때까지 닥치는 대로 다 시도해 보다. '안 되면 말고' 식으로 여러 방법을 동시에 던져보는 것
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase when describing a rapid, multi-pronged approach to a launch, campaign, or sales push where the team hasn't yet identified the single winning method.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is describing his aggressive, experimental go-to-market approach when launching Agentforce — Salesforce's AI agent platform. Rather than committing to a single strategy upfront, he tests many tactics simultaneously to discover what resonates with customers.

vocabulary.meaning: a specific action or method used to achieve a short-term goal
vocabulary.note: Distinguished from 'strategy,' which is the broader plan; tactics are the individual moves within that plan.

cards.sentence: I introduced it at our Dreamforce Conference, and that was one way to break through, which was I took our conference and said, "It's just going to be about Agentforce."

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 소음 속에서 치고 나가 대중의 주목을 확실히 사로잡다
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase when describing a pivotal moment — a launch event, announcement, or campaign — that finally gave a product or idea the visibility it needed.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is explaining how he used Salesforce's annual Dreamforce conference as a platform to generate massive awareness for Agentforce, the company's new AI product. By dedicating the entire event to a single product, he found a way to stand out in a crowded market.

cards.sentence: I'm trying to think about, "What are all the things I need to do to get my company 100% on Agentforce, my customers, everyone?" because I know I have a window of opportunity here.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 지금만 열려 있는 기회의 창. 놓치면 닫혀버리는 호기(好機)
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase in pitches, strategy discussions, or board updates to convey that a competitive advantage or market timing is real but time-limited.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is describing the urgency he feels around adopting AI agents across Salesforce. He believes the company is in a defining moment where early, all-in commitment to the technology will determine who wins the next era of enterprise software.

cards.sentence: I am looking to try to find the winning tactic and turn it into a winning strategy.

keyPhrase.text: turn a tactic into a strategy
keyPhrase.surface: find the winning tactic and turn it into a winning strategy
keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 효과가 증명된 단발 전술 하나를 골라 반복 가능한 전략 체계로 키워내다
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase in strategy or post-mortem discussions when the team has found early proof of something working and needs to shift from experimentation to systematic execution.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is discussing his product-launch philosophy for Agentforce: he intentionally experiments with many individual moves — events, messaging angles, partnerships — to find the one that gets traction, then builds the entire go-to-market motion around it.

vocabulary.meaning: a specific action or method used to achieve a short-term goal
vocabulary.note: Contrasted here with 'strategy,' the broader repeatable plan built from proven tactics.

cards.sentence: I have a pretty deep meditation practice for three, four decades which is we have to be cultivating our beginner's mind.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 기존의 고정관념과 경험을 의도적으로 비워, 처음 맞닥뜨리는 사람처럼 열린 자세를 갖추다. '초심(初心)을 기르다'
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase in leadership, innovation, or coaching conversations to encourage a team to set aside entrenched assumptions and approach a problem with fresh eyes.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is sharing the mental discipline he draws from decades of meditation practice. He argues that leaders must actively unlearn assumptions and approach fast-changing situations — like the AI revolution — without being anchored to past playbooks.

vocabulary.meaning: deliberately developing or nurturing a quality over time through consistent practice

cards.sentence: You want to be playing the whole symphony, and you want to get everyone running. That symphony is sales, service, marketing, product.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 회사의 모든 기능(영업·마케팅·제품 등)을 빠짐없이 한 박자로 움직이게 조율하다
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this metaphor in all-hands meetings, QBRs, or strategy sessions to emphasize cross-functional alignment and warn against siloed execution.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is advising founders not to over-index on one function — such as product or sales — but to align every part of the company simultaneously. He uses the metaphor of a symphony orchestra to illustrate that every instrument (department) must play in concert for the company to perform at its best.

vocabulary.meaning: an elaborate musical composition for a full orchestra; here used metaphorically for a complex, coordinated business effort

cards.sentence: If you're going to narrowcast yourself, you're doing a disservice not just to yourself, but to everybody else as well.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 리더로서의 역할을 한 영역에만 가둬 스스로 영향력을 좁혀버리다
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this term to challenge leaders who are overindexing on one area (e.g., only product, only sales) and avoiding the full scope of their responsibilities.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is warning founders and executives against becoming specialists who only manage one domain. In his view, a CEO who focuses exclusively on product — or any single function — fails both their team and the company by neglecting the full system of leadership.

vocabulary.meaning: to transmit or direct something to a small, specific audience; here adapted metaphorically to mean constraining one's leadership reach
vocabulary.note: Originally a media term (opposite of 'broadcast'); Benioff uses it as a leadership metaphor.

vocabulary.meaning: an action that harms or fails someone who deserved better

cards.sentence: Then, when we did the layoff, then I'm trying to overcommunicate. I'm having all employee meetings. It's a complete dumpster fire.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 손 쓸 수 없을 만큼 완전히 엉망이 된 상황. 재앙에 가까운 혼돈
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this informal phrase in candid retrospectives or post-mortems to describe a period when multiple things went wrong at once and the situation felt unmanageable.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is recounting the turbulent period at Salesforce following major layoffs. Despite his efforts to communicate openly with employees through all-hands meetings, the internal atmosphere was one of overwhelming disorder and morale collapse.

vocabulary.meaning: to communicate far more frequently and thoroughly than might feel necessary, often to prevent confusion during a crisis

cards.sentence: There's no up-and-to-the-right perfect chart where it's just one line. I don't care who you are. Apple doesn't have one. No one has one.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 끊김 없이 우상향하는 완벽한 성장 곡선. 현실에선 누구도 갖지 못하는 이상적 그래프
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase in investor updates, team retrospectives, or leadership talks to reset expectations and normalize the non-linear reality of building a company.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is pushing back against the myth of flawless, linear company growth. He argues that every company — even Apple — experiences downturns, pivots, and setbacks, and that expecting a perfectly smooth growth curve is an unrealistic and unhealthy standard.

cards.sentence: the entrepreneurial life is a rock-and-roll roller coaster, and you get ready because it's going to be pounding you all the time.

keyPhrase.meaning_ko: 창업의 여정은 극단적인 부침이 쉼 없이 몰아치는 롤러코스터라는 것. 쾌감과 고통이 번갈아 강타한다
keyPhrase.usageTip: Use this phrase in founder talks, investor pitches, or mentorship conversations to honestly frame the emotional and operational intensity of building a company.

cards.context: Marc Benioff is offering a candid reality check to aspiring founders: entrepreneurship is not a steady climb but a relentless, exhilarating, and often brutal ride. He draws on his own decades at Salesforce to frame volatility and pressure as inherent features of the path, not signs of failure.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to the activity of setting up and running a business, accepting financial risk in the hope of profit

speaker.name: Marc Benioff
speaker.title: Co-founder & CEO, Salesforce
speaker.motif: a spinning mandala overlaid with interlocking gears, each rotating at different speeds yet creating harmonious motion
speaker.introLine: Welcome, seeker. I have built mountains from dreams, and I have razed them down again. Through meditation and chaos alike, I learned that the true art is orchestrating the whole symphony—knowing when to throw boldly and when to listen deeply to what sticks.
speaker.sealLine: Remember: the entrepreneurial path is never a perfect line upward, but a rock-and-roll journey. Master the beginner's mind, and you will turn each tactic into timeless strategy.
